title: Add learnSteps to Payment System template (idempotency, ledger, settlement)
description: Payment System template (payment-system.json) has zero learnSteps despite difficulty 4. This is a P0 content bug — users get architecture with no educational scaffolding. Add 4 learnSteps teaching: (1) Idempotency key strategy (UUID deduplication window), (2) Exactly-once semantics (transactional outbox pattern), (3) Double-entry ledger accounting (every credit has a matching debit), (4) Settlement flow (authorization → clearing → settlement, 1-3 day cycle). Also add missing architectural elements: settlement service, fraud detection service, update edges for the full payment lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 learnSteps added explaining idempotency, exactly-once, ledger accounting, and settlement
Each step highlights relevant nodes and explains WHY the pattern exists
Settlement service and fraud detection nodes added to architecture
Real-world accuracy improved to match actual payment processing flow

title: Add learnSteps to Instagram template (feed generation, social graph, media pipeline)
description: Instagram template (instagram.json) has zero learnSteps despite difficulty 5. Add 4-5 learnSteps teaching: (1) Feed generation strategy (fanout-on-write vs fanout-on-read hybrid), (2) Social graph partitioning and follower-following relationships, (3) Media upload pipeline (client → CDN → transcode → multiple quality variants), (4) Feed ranking (engagement-based vs chronological), (5) Private account access control.
acceptanceCriteria: 4-5 learnSteps added covering feed, social graph, media pipeline, and ranking
Steps explain WHY each approach was chosen over alternatives
Feed ranking algorithm difference (chronological vs engagement) explicitly taught

title: Add learnSteps to Notification System + fix simulation node references
description: Notification System template has zero learnSteps AND simulation chaos scenarios reference non-existent nodes (dedup-service, push-provider). Fix both: (1) Add 4 learnSteps teaching priority queuing, deduplication strategy, rate limiting per channel, delivery tracking with retries. (2) Fix simulation to reference actual node IDs from the template.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 learnSteps added covering priority queuing, dedup, rate limiting, and delivery tracking
All simulation chaos scenario node references match actual template node IDs
No references to non-existent nodes remain

title: Add learnSteps to Search Engine template
description: Search Engine template (search-engine.json) has zero learnSteps despite difficulty 4. Add 4 learnSteps teaching: (1) Crawling and indexing pipeline, (2) Inverted index data structure and relevance scoring (TF-IDF, BM25), (3) Query processing (tokenization, stemming, spell correction), (4) PageRank or link analysis for ranking.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 learnSteps added covering crawling, indexing, query processing, and ranking
Steps explain relevance scoring (BM25) and why PageRank matters
Beginner-friendly language used throughout

title: Fix Twitter simulation referencing non-existent fanout-workers node
description: twitter-fanout.json simulation chaos scenarios and cost breakdown reference 'fanout-workers' node ID, but the actual template uses 'fanout-service'. Update all simulation references to match actual node IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: All simulation node references match actual template node IDs
Simulation runs without errors on the Twitter template
No orphan node references remain

title: Create Proximity/Location Service template (Yelp/Nearby Places)
description: CRITICAL gap: #1 missing template. All competitors have it. Top-5 FAANG interview question. Tests geospatial indexing (Geohash, Quadtree, S2/H3), proximity search, ranking by distance+relevance, geographic data partitioning. Nodes: mobile-client, api-gw, location-service, geospatial-index-service, places-db, cache, search-engine, CDN. learnSteps: (1) Geospatial indexing approaches — Geohash vs Quadtree vs S2 cells, (2) Proximity search — expanding rings with distance threshold, (3) Ranking — combining distance with relevance/ratings/freshness, (4) Scaling — geographic partitioning and caching hot regions. Simulation with chaos: cold region cache miss, DB partition hotspot, stale location data. Sources: Alex Xu Vol 2 Ch 1, HelloInterview Yelp, DesignGurus Proximity Service.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 8+ nodes with correct geospatial architecture
learnSteps explain Geohash vs Quadtree vs S2 with trade-offs
Simulation config with 3+ chaos scenarios
Difficulty rating: 3
Template loads and simulates correctly

title: Create File Storage/Sync template (Dropbox/Google Drive)
description: CRITICAL gap: Top-10 interview question. All competitors have it. Tests chunking, deduplication (content-addressable), sync conflict resolution, versioning, large file handling. Nodes: web-client, mobile-client, api-gw, upload-service, chunk-service, dedup-service, metadata-db, object-storage (S3), sync-service, notification-service, CDN. learnSteps: (1) Chunked upload — why chunk, optimal chunk size, resumable uploads, (2) Content-addressable deduplication — SHA-256 hash per chunk, ~40% storage savings, (3) Sync conflict resolution — OT vs CRDT vs last-writer-wins, (4) Versioning — snapshot vs delta storage trade-offs. Simulation with chaos: large file upload timeout, concurrent edit conflict, storage quota exceeded. Sources: Alex Xu Vol 2 Ch 14 (Google Drive), HelloInterview Dropbox, DesignGurus Dropbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering upload, sync, dedup, and storage paths
learnSteps explain chunking, dedup, conflict resolution, versioning
Simulation config with 3+ chaos scenarios
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create Google Maps / Navigation System template
description: CRITICAL gap: Frequent FAANG question (Google literally asks this). Tests graph algorithms (Dijkstra/A*), geospatial data, tile rendering, ETA computation, real-time traffic. Nodes: mobile-client, api-gw, routing-service, tile-server, traffic-service, geocoding-service, search-engine, road-graph-db, cache, CDN. learnSteps: (1) Road graph representation — nodes as intersections, edges as road segments with weights, (2) Shortest path with traffic — Dijkstra/A* with real-time weight updates, (3) Tile-based rendering — zoom levels, tile coordinates, caching strategy, (4) ETA computation — historical patterns + real-time traffic + ML prediction. Sources: Alex Xu Vol 2 Ch 3 (Google Maps), Codemia Google Maps problem.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering navigation architecture
learnSteps explain graph-based routing, tile rendering, ETA computation
Simulation config with scenarios: traffic jam re-routing, tile cache miss
Difficulty rating: 5

title: Create TikTok / Short-Video Platform template
description: CRITICAL gap: #1 NEW interview question 2025-2026. Codemia is the only competitor with it. Tests recommendation engine + video upload pipeline + feed generation + CDN delivery + content moderation. Nodes: mobile-client, api-gw, upload-service, video-transcoding-service, CDN, recommendation-engine, feed-service, user-db, content-moderation, analytics-service, message-queue. learnSteps: (1) Video processing pipeline — upload, transcode to multiple resolutions, generate thumbnails, (2) Recommendation algorithm — collaborative filtering + content-based + engagement signals, (3) Feed generation — pull model with ranking at read-time, (4) Content moderation — async pipeline with ML classification + human review. Sources: Educative Design TikTok, SystemDesignHandbook TikTok guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering full short-video platform
learnSteps explain recommendation, video processing, feed gen, moderation
Simulation config with: viral video spike, cold-start recommendation
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create Design ChatGPT / LLM Service template
description: CRITICAL gap: Standard interview question at AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta) in 2026. Educative lists it as a medium-hard question. Tests streaming response delivery (SSE), model routing, context management, token-level billing, rate limiting per user. Nodes: web-client, api-gw, request-router, model-server (GPT-4/Claude), context-manager, token-counter, rate-limiter, conversation-store, cache (prompt cache), monitoring. learnSteps: (1) Streaming response delivery — SSE with token-by-token output, (2) Model routing — tier selection based on complexity (fast/cheap model for simple queries, expensive model for complex), (3) Context management — conversation history + system prompt + token window limits, (4) Cost optimization — prompt caching, batching, and quantization. Sources: Educative Design ChatGPT, GenAI System Design Guide 2026, SystemDesignHandbook AI System Design.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering LLM service architecture
learnSteps explain streaming, model routing, context management, cost optimization
Simulation config with: high concurrency, model timeout, token budget exceeded
Uses existing AI/LLM component types (llm-gateway, etc.)
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create RAG Pipeline Architecture template
description: CRITICAL gap + FIRST-MOVER advantage: Most-asked AI system design question in 2026. 70%+ enterprise gen-AI uses RAG. NO competitor has an interactive version. Nodes: web-client, api-gw, query-service, embedding-service, vector-db, document-store, llm-gateway, reranker-service, chunking-pipeline, ingestion-service, cache, monitoring. learnSteps: (1) Document ingestion — parsing, chunking strategies (fixed-size, semantic, recursive), metadata extraction, (2) Embedding and indexing — embedding models (text-embedding-3), HNSW indexing, dimensionality trade-offs, (3) Retrieval — vector similarity search + BM25 keyword search (hybrid), reranking with cross-encoder, (4) Generation — prompt assembly with retrieved context, hallucination mitigation, citation generation, (5) Monitoring — retrieval precision, generation quality, semantic drift detection. Sources: n8n RAG Architecture Guide, Techment 10 RAG Architectures, Cloudian RAG Components.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering full RAG pipeline
learnSteps explain chunking, embedding, hybrid retrieval, reranking, generation
Simulation config with: large corpus ingestion, semantic drift, model latency spike
Uses existing AI/LLM components (llm-gateway, memory-fabric, etc.)
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create Multi-Region Architecture template
description: CRITICAL gap: Senior+ FAANG question. No competitor has this. Tests active-active vs active-passive, cross-region replication, failover routing, data sovereignty/GDPR. Nodes across 3 regions: clients (US, EU, Asia), DNS with geo-routing, CDN per region, api-gw per region, app-servers per region, primary-db (US), replica-db (EU, Asia), cache per region, cross-region message-queue. learnSteps: (1) Active-Active vs Active-Passive — trade-offs (complexity vs failover speed), (2) Cross-region replication — async vs sync, replication lag, conflict resolution, (3) Data residency and GDPR — what data must stay in-region, (4) Failover strategies — DNS-based routing, health checks, Regional Isolation with Shared Services pattern. Sources: OneUpTime Multi-Region Guide, DasRoot AWS Multi-Region Patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 12+ nodes across 3 geographic regions
Visual layout groups nodes by region
learnSteps explain active-active/passive, replication lag, GDPR
Simulation config with: region failure, cross-region latency spike
Difficulty rating: 5

title: Create Vector Database Architecture template
description: IMPORTANT gap: Core AI infrastructure. 30%+ enterprise adoption. Tests HNSW indexing, embedding pipelines, ANN search, storage-compute separation, hybrid search. Nodes: api-gw, embedding-service, vector-index (HNSW), metadata-store, object-storage, query-router, cache, monitoring. learnSteps: (1) HNSW algorithm — hierarchical graph for fast ANN, recall vs speed trade-off, (2) Indexing strategies — HNSW vs IVF vs PQ, when to use each, (3) Hybrid search — combining vector similarity + BM25 keyword, (4) Scaling — sharding vectors by partition, replication for fault tolerance. Sources: Pinecone docs, Dev.to Vector DB architecture, OneUpTime Vector DB guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 8+ nodes covering vector DB architecture
learnSteps explain HNSW, indexing strategies, hybrid search
Simulation config with: high-dimensional query latency, index rebuild
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create Agentic AI System Design template
description: EMERGING gap: Hottest 2026 topic. CNCF Agentics Day. Gartner: 40% enterprise adoption. 15 design patterns identified. Architex already has agent-orchestrator, tool-registry, memory-fabric, safety-mesh components — just needs a template. Nodes: web-client, api-gw, agent-orchestrator, llm-gateway, tool-registry, memory-fabric, safety-mesh, vector-db, function-executor, monitoring. learnSteps: (1) ReAct pattern — Reason+Act loop, when to think vs when to act, (2) Tool calling — schema definition, sandboxed execution, timeout handling, (3) Memory systems — short-term (context), working (scratchpad), long-term (vector DB), (4) Safety and guardrails — input filtering, output validation, hallucination detection, (5) Multi-agent coordination — orchestrator vs swarm patterns. Sources: Google Cloud Agentic AI Guide, SitePoint Agentic Patterns 2026, Anthropic Agent SDK docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Template uses existing AI/LLM components (agent-orchestrator, tool-registry, etc.)
learnSteps explain ReAct, tool calling, memory, safety, multi-agent
Simulation config with: agent loop, tool timeout, safety violation
Difficulty rating: 5

title: Create Airbnb / Marketplace Platform template
description: IMPORTANT gap: Two-sided marketplace commonly asked at Airbnb, Amazon, Meta. Tests search ranking, booking with distributed locks, dynamic pricing, reviews, dual-sided marketplace. Nodes: web-client, api-gw, search-service, listing-service, booking-service, payment-gateway, review-service, pricing-service, database, cache, message-queue, notification-service. learnSteps: (1) Two-sided marketplace dynamics — matching supply/demand, network effects, (2) Search ranking — combining relevance, price, availability, host quality, (3) Booking with distributed locks — preventing double-booking, optimistic locking, (4) Dynamic pricing — demand-based pricing, seasonality, competitor comparison. Sources: Codemia Airbnb, HelloInterview marketplace patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering marketplace architecture
learnSteps explain marketplace dynamics, search ranking, booking locks, pricing
Simulation config with: double-booking race condition, search ranking bias
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create Ad Click Aggregator template
description: IMPORTANT gap: HelloInterview has it. Commonly asked at Meta/Google. Tests real-time event aggregation, exactly-once counting at scale, lambda/kappa architecture. Nodes: client, api-gw, event-collector, stream-processor (Flink), aggregation-service, timeseries-db, cache, dashboard-service, message-queue (Kafka). learnSteps: (1) Event ingestion at scale — millions of clicks/sec, partitioned by ad_id, (2) Exactly-once aggregation — idempotent processors, dedup with event IDs, (3) Lambda vs Kappa architecture — speed layer + batch layer vs stream-only, (4) Querying aggregated data — rollup tables, pre-computed materialized views. Sources: HelloInterview Ad Click Aggregator, Alex Xu Vol 2 Ch 6.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 8+ nodes covering ad click aggregation pipeline
learnSteps explain event ingestion, exactly-once, lambda vs kappa
Simulation config with: event burst, consumer lag, partition rebalance
Difficulty rating: 4

title: Create LLM Serving Infrastructure template
description: EMERGING gap: Production AI infrastructure — vLLM, continuous batching, PagedAttention, KV cache, disaggregated serving. Nodes: api-gw, rate-limiter, model-router, prefill-worker, decode-worker, kv-cache-manager, model-registry, gpu-cluster, monitoring, autoscaler. learnSteps: (1) Continuous batching — vs static batching, throughput improvement, (2) PagedAttention — virtual memory for KV cache, memory efficiency, (3) Disaggregated serving — separating prefill from decode for cost optimization, (4) Quantization — FP16 → FP8 → INT4, accuracy vs speed trade-off, (5) GPU selection — H100 vs A10G, inference vs training. Sources: SystemDesignHandbook LLM System Design, RunPod AI Model Serving, llm-d architecture docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes 10+ nodes covering LLM serving stack
learnSteps explain continuous batching, PagedAttention, disaggregated serving, quantization
Simulation config with: GPU saturation, cold model load, KV cache overflow
Difficulty rating: 5

title: Implement FAANG Interview Prep learning path (4-week curriculum)
description: Build a structured 4-week learning path based on 2026 interview patterns. Week 1: Foundations (7 concept topics). Week 2: Core Problems (7 most-asked templates). Week 3: Advanced Problems (7 harder templates). Week 4: 2026 Topics + Mock Practice. Each day maps to specific templates/concepts. Path tracks completion progress. Based on: NeetCode roadmap, ByteByteGo top 20, Alex Xu Vol 1+2, HelloInterview patterns.

Progress tracking: days completed, templates studied, simulations run
Prerequisites shown between topics
Can be followed without skipping ahead

title: Implement AI/ML Engineer learning path (2026 edition)
description: Build a structured learning path for AI/ML engineers covering system design for AI applications. 8 modules: (1) ML Pipeline/MLOps, (2) RAG Pipeline Architecture, (3) Vector Database System, (4) LLM Serving Infrastructure, (5) Agentic AI System Design, (6) Design ChatGPT Service, (7) Recommendation Engine, (8) Content Moderation. This is the BIGGEST opportunity — no competitor has this path.

title: Add Back-of-Envelope Estimation interactive calculator to system design module
description: Every SD interview starts with estimation. No interactive tool exists for this. Build a calculator panel that helps users estimate: QPS from DAU (DAU → peak QPS conversion), storage requirements (users × data per user × retention), bandwidth (QPS × payload size), server count (QPS ÷ capacity per server). Include reference numbers card with 2026-accurate hardware specs (modern server = 500K+ RPS for simple endpoints, SSD = 3GB/s, etc.). Warn when users use outdated numbers. Sources: ByteByteGo Back-of-Envelope, DesignGurus estimation guide.
acceptanceCriteria: Estimation calculator accessible from toolbar or bottom panel
Supports: QPS, storage, bandwidth, and server count estimations
Reference card with 2026-accurate hardware numbers
Calculations feed into simulation traffic config when available
Warning when numbers seem unrealistic (too high or using outdated benchmarks)

title: Add difficulty tags and prerequisite graph to all 55+ templates
description: Templates have difficulty ratings (1-5) in JSON but these are not visually communicated as skill levels. Add: (1) Difficulty tags (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced/Expert) on each template card and on the canvas. (2) Prerequisite graph — e.g., 'Learn URL Shortener before Chat System' or 'Learn Distributed Cache before designing Netflix.' Prerequisites can be derived from shared concepts (caching pattern appears in URL Shortener first, then in Netflix). Sources: Reddit 'what order to study system design', NeetCode roadmap.
acceptanceCriteria: Every template shows skill level tag (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced/Expert)
Prerequisite suggestions shown when user opens a template above their current level
Prerequisite graph viewable from template gallery
Suggestions based on concepts taught in earlier templates

title: Add 'When To Use' comparison guides for technology categories
description: Students struggle with 'Why Kafka not RabbitMQ?' and 'SQL vs NoSQL' decisions. Build comparison guide cards accessible from the properties panel when selecting components. Categories: (1) Databases: SQL vs NoSQL vs NewSQL vs Graph vs TimeSeries vs Vector, (2) Message Queues: Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs Pulsar vs SQS, (3) Caches: Redis vs Memcached, (4) APIs: REST vs gRPC vs GraphQL, (5) Storage: Block vs Object vs File, (6) LBs: L4 vs L7. Each comparison shows: when to use, when NOT to use, scaling characteristics, cost implications. Sources: ByteByteGo comparison posts, DesignGurus 'when to use' guides.
acceptanceCriteria: 6 comparison guides built (databases, queues, caches, APIs, storage, LBs)
Accessible from properties panel when a relevant component is selected
Each guide shows when-to-use, when-not-to-use, and scaling characteristics
Written in interviewer-friendly language (good for articulating trade-offs)

title: Add real-world application context to top 20 templates
description: Templates teach architecture but not real-world context. For each of the top 20 templates, add: (1) 'Who uses this?' section naming 3+ real companies that use this pattern, (2) 'Real numbers' section with production-scale metrics (e.g., 'Twitter processes 500M tweets/day', 'Netflix serves 15% of global internet traffic'), (3) 'Why was it built this way?' section with the historical engineering decision. Sources: Engineering blogs (Netflix Tech Blog, Uber Engineering, Meta Engineering), ByteByteGo case studies.
acceptanceCriteria: Top 20 templates have real-world context sections
